title: Rotate Array
questionSummary: Given an array, rotate the array to the right by k steps, where k is non-negative.
solutionSummary: Use a temporary array to store the rotated elements, or reverse the array three times to achieve O(1) space complexity.

title: Evaluate Reverse Polish Notation
questionSummary: Evaluate the value of an arithmetic expression in Reverse Polish Notation. Valid operators are +, -, *, /.
solutionSummary: Use a Stack. Iterate through each element: if it's a number, push to stack; if an operator, pop two numbers, apply the operation, and push the result back.

title: Longest Palindromic Substring
questionSummary: Given a string s, find the longest palindromic substring in s. A palindrome reads the same backward as forward.
solutionSummary: Approach 1 (Dynamic Programming): Store palindrome status of substrings in a 2D table, taking O(N^2) time and space. Approach 2 (Optimal): Expand around each character/gap as centers, taking O(N^2) time and O(1) space.

title: Word Break
questionSummary: Given a string s and a dictionary of words, determine if s can be segmented into a space-separated sequence of one or more dictionary words.
solutionSummary: Use Dynamic Programming. Maintain a boolean array dp where dp[i] is true if the prefix of length i can be segmented.

title: Word Break II
questionSummary: Given a string s and a dictionary of words, add spaces in s to construct a sentence where each word is a valid dictionary word. Return all such possible sentences.
solutionSummary: Use DFS with memoization to find all possible paths.

title: Word Ladder
questionSummary: Given two words (start and end), and a dictionary, find the length of shortest transformation sequence from start to end.
solutionSummary: Use Breadth-First Search (BFS). Push the start word to a queue and transform characters one by one, checking if the new word is in the dictionary.

title: Median of Two Sorted Arrays Java
questionSummary: There are two sorted arrays A and B of size m and n respectively. Find the median of the two sorted arrays.
solutionSummary: Use Binary Search to partition both arrays such that the left half has the same number of elements as the right half.

title: Regular Expression Matching
questionSummary: Implement regular expression matching with support for '.' and '*'.
solutionSummary: Use Dynamic Programming. dp[i][j] represents if the first i characters of s match the first j characters of p.

title: Merge Intervals
questionSummary: Given a collection of intervals, merge all overlapping intervals.
solutionSummary: Sort the intervals based on the start time, then iterate and merge overlapping intervals.

title: Insert Interval
questionSummary: Given a set of non-overlapping & sorted intervals, insert a new interval into the intervals (merge if necessary).
solutionSummary: Iterate through intervals, add non-overlapping ones, and merge overlapping ones with the new interval.

title: Two Sum
questionSummary: Given an array of integers, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number.
solutionSummary: Use a HashMap to store the numbers and their indices. For each number, check if target - number exists in the map.

title: Two Sum II Input array is sorted
questionSummary: Given a 1-indexed sorted array of integers, find two numbers such that they add up to a specific target number. Return their indices.
solutionSummary: Use a two-pointer approach. Initialize one pointer at the start and one at the end of the array. If the sum is smaller than target, move the left pointer right; if larger, move the right pointer left; otherwise, return the pointers. O(N) time, O(1) space.

title: Two Sum III Data structure design
questionSummary: Design and implement a TwoSum class with add and find operations.
solutionSummary: Use a HashMap to store the frequencies of added numbers. For the find operation, iterate through the keys and check if target - key exists.

title: 3Sum
questionSummary: Find all unique triplets in the array which gives the sum of zero.
solutionSummary: Sort the array, then iterate. For each element, use two pointers (left and right) to find pairs that sum to the negative of the current element.

title: 4Sum
questionSummary: Given an array S of n integers, find all unique quadruplets in S which gives the sum of target.
solutionSummary: Sort the array and use two nested loops to fix the first two elements, then use two pointers for the remaining two elements.

title: 3Sum Closest
questionSummary: Given an array S of n integers, find three integers in S such that the sum is closest to a given target.
solutionSummary: Sort the array. Iterate through it, and use two pointers to find the sum closest to the target, keeping track of the minimum difference.

title: String to Integer (atoi)
questionSummary: Implement atoi to convert a string to an integer.
solutionSummary: Trim whitespaces, handle the sign, and iterate through digits while checking for overflow/underflow against Integer.MAX_VALUE and Integer.MIN_VALUE.

title: Merge Sorted Array
questionSummary: Given two sorted integer arrays A and B, merge B into A as one sorted array.
solutionSummary: Use three pointers starting from the end of arrays A and B to place the largest elements at the end of A without overwriting.

title: Valid Parentheses
questionSummary: Given a string containing just the characters '(', ')', '{', '}', '[' and ']', determine if the input string is valid.
solutionSummary: Use a Stack. Push opening brackets and for closing brackets, check if they match the popped top of the stack.

title: Implement strStr
questionSummary: Returns a pointer to the first occurrence of needle in haystack, or null if needle is not part of haystack.
solutionSummary: Use two nested loops to compare substrings, or apply KMP algorithm for O(N) time complexity.

title: Set Matrix Zeroes
questionSummary: Given a m x n matrix, if an element is 0, set its entire row and column to 0. Do it in place.
solutionSummary: Use the first row and first column to keep track of which rows and columns need to be set to zero.

title: Search Insert Position
questionSummary: Given a sorted array and a target value, return the index if the target is found. If not, return the index where it would be if it were inserted in order.
solutionSummary: Use Binary Search to find the target or the insertion point in O(log N) time.

title: Longest Consecutive Sequence Java
questionSummary: Given an unsorted array of integers, find the length of the longest consecutive elements sequence.
solutionSummary: Use a HashSet. For each number, if it's the start of a sequence (i.e., num-1 is not in set), count the consecutive numbers.

title: Valid Palindrome
questionSummary: Given a string, determine if it is a palindrome, considering only alphanumeric characters and ignoring cases.
solutionSummary: Use two pointers from the start and end, skipping non-alphanumeric characters, and compare them.

title: Spiral Matrix
questionSummary: Given a matrix of m x n elements, return all elements of the matrix in spiral order.
solutionSummary: Keep track of four boundaries (top, bottom, left, right) and traverse the perimeter, shrinking the boundaries inward.

title: Search a 2D Matrix
questionSummary: Write an efficient algorithm that searches for a value in an m x n matrix where rows are sorted and the first integer of each row is greater than the last of the previous.
solutionSummary: Treat the 2D matrix as a 1D sorted array and apply Binary Search.

title: Rotate Image
questionSummary: You are given an n x n 2D matrix representing an image. Rotate the image by 90 degrees (clockwise).
solutionSummary: Reverse the matrix top to bottom, then swap the symmetry (transpose).

title: Triangle
questionSummary: Given a triangle, find the minimum path sum from top to bottom.
solutionSummary: Use bottom-up Dynamic Programming. Update each element with the minimum of its two adjacent children in the row below.

title: Distinct Subsequences Total
questionSummary: Given a string S and a string T, count the number of distinct subsequences of T in S.
solutionSummary: Use Dynamic Programming. dp[i][j] represents the number of distinct subsequences of T[0..j] in S[0..i].

title: Maximum Subarray
questionSummary: Find the contiguous subarray within an array which has the largest sum.
solutionSummary: Use Kadane's Algorithm. Keep track of the current subarray sum, resetting to zero if it becomes negative, and update the max sum.

title: Maximum Product Subarray
questionSummary: Find the contiguous subarray within an array which has the largest product.
solutionSummary: Keep track of both the maximum and minimum products ending at each position, as a negative number can flip the minimum to maximum.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array
questionSummary: Given a sorted array, remove the duplicates in place such that each element appears only once and return the new length.
solutionSummary: Use two pointers. One pointer iterates through the array, and the other keeps track of the position of unique elements.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted Array II
questionSummary: Follow up for 'Remove Duplicates': What if duplicates are allowed at most twice?
solutionSummary: Use two pointers. Allow an element if it's different from the element two steps back in the modified array.

title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
questionSummary: Given a string, find the length of the longest substring without repeating characters.
solutionSummary: Use a sliding window with a HashSet or HashMap to track characters and their indices, updating the window when a duplicate is found.

title: Longest Substring Which Contains 2 Unique Characters
questionSummary: Given a string, find the longest substring that contains at most two unique characters.
solutionSummary: Use a sliding window and a HashMap to track character frequencies, shrinking the window when there are more than two distinct characters.

title: Palindrome Partitioning
questionSummary: Given a string s, partition s such that every substring of the partition is a palindrome. Return all possible palindrome partitioning configurations.
solutionSummary: Use backtracking (depth-first search) to generate all partitions. At each step, if the prefix is a palindrome, recursively partition the remaining substring. Backtrack and try other split points.

title: Reverse Words in a String
questionSummary: Given an input string, reverse the string word by word.
solutionSummary: Split the string by spaces, reverse the array of words, and join them back with a single space.

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array
questionSummary: Suppose a sorted array is rotated at some pivot. Find the minimum element.
solutionSummary: Use Binary Search. Compare the middle element with the rightmost element to determine which half contains the minimum.

title: Find Minimum in Rotated Sorted Array II
questionSummary: Given a sorted array rotated at some pivot, find the minimum element. The array may contain duplicates, which makes finding the pivot more complex.
solutionSummary: Use a modified Binary Search. If mid-value equals high-value, we cannot decide which half is sorted; decrement the high pointer by one. Otherwise, follow standard rotated binary search. Worst case time is O(N) due to duplicates.

title: Find Peak Element
questionSummary: A peak element is an element that is greater than its neighbors. Given an input array, find a peak element and return its index.
solutionSummary: Use Binary Search. Compare the middle element with its right neighbor to decide which half to explore.

title: Min Stack
questionSummary: Design a stack that supports push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element in constant time.
solutionSummary: Use two stacks: one for the regular elements and one to keep track of the minimum values at each step.

title: Majority Element
questionSummary: Given an array of size n, find the majority element which appears more than n/2 times.
solutionSummary: Use Boyer-Moore Voting Algorithm. Maintain a candidate and a counter, incrementing for matches and decrementing for mismatches.

title: Combination Sum
questionSummary: Given a set of candidate numbers and a target number, find all unique combinations where the candidates sum to target.
solutionSummary: Use Backtracking (DFS). Recursively subtract the candidate from target and explore combinations.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock
questionSummary: Say you have an array for which the ith element is the price of a given stock on day i. Find the maximum profit for at most one transaction.
solutionSummary: Keep track of the minimum price seen so far and calculate the maximum profit possible at each step.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock II
questionSummary: Find the maximum profit where you can complete as many transactions as you like (buy one and sell one share of the stock multiple times).
solutionSummary: Iterate through the array and accumulate profit for any adjacent days where the price increases.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock III
questionSummary: Find the maximum profit for at most two transactions.
solutionSummary: Use Dynamic Programming. Maintain two arrays for max profit from left to right and max profit from right to left, then combine them.

title: Best Time to Buy and Sell Stock IV
questionSummary: Given stock prices, find the maximum profit you can achieve by completing at most k transactions.
solutionSummary: Use Dynamic Programming. If k is large (>= N/2), solve as unlimited transactions. Otherwise, maintain a 2D table dp[i][j] representing the max profit at day j with at most i transactions. Time complexity is O(k * N).

title: Longest Common Prefix
questionSummary: Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings. If there is no common prefix, return an empty string.
solutionSummary: Sort the array of strings and compare the first and last strings character by character, or sequentially match the prefix against all strings, updating the common prefix length.

title: Largest Number
questionSummary: Given a list of non-negative integers, arrange them such that they form the largest number. Since the result can be very large, return it as a string.
solutionSummary: Sort the integers using a custom comparator that compares two numbers concatenated in different orders: if ab > ba, then a comes before b. Concatenate the sorted numbers to form the final result.

title: Combinations
questionSummary: Given two integers n and k, return all possible combinations of k numbers chosen from the range 1 to n.
solutionSummary: Use backtracking (DFS) to explore combinations. Maintain a path of current numbers, recursively add the next larger number, and backtrack when the path length reaches k.

title: Compare Version Numbers
questionSummary: Compare two version numbers version1 and version2. Return -1, 0, or 1 based on which version is greater.
solutionSummary: Split both version strings by the dot character. Iterate through the segments, convert each to an integer, and compare them. Pad shorter versions with 0s if necessary. Time complexity is O(N + M).

title: Gas Station
questionSummary: Given gas and cost arrays representing gas stations along a circular route, find the starting station's index if you can travel around the circuit once in the clockwise direction.
solutionSummary: Maintain total_gas and current_gas. If current_gas falls below zero at station i, reset the starting station to i + 1 and reset current_gas to 0. If total_gas >= 0 at the end, return the start index.

title: Candy
questionSummary: Given children ratings, distribute candies such that each child has at least one candy and children with higher ratings than neighbors get more candies. Find the minimum candies required.
solutionSummary: Use a two-pass greedy approach. Iterate left-to-right, ensuring rating increases get more candies than the left neighbor. Then iterate right-to-left, ensuring right neighbor conditions are met. Sum the maximum candies allocated.

title: Jump Game
questionSummary: Given an array of non-negative integers representing max jump lengths, determine if you are able to reach the last index starting from index 0.
solutionSummary: Maintain a max_reach variable representing the furthest index reachable. Iterate through the array; if the current index is reachable, update max_reach = max(max_reach, i + nums[i]). Return true if max_reach reaches the last index.

title: Pascal's Triangle
questionSummary: Given a non-negative integer numRows, generate the first numRows of Pascal's triangle, where each number is the sum of the two numbers directly above it.
solutionSummary: Iterate row by row. For each row, set the first and last elements to 1, and compute the middle elements as the sum of elements at indices j-1 and j in the previous row. Time complexity is O(N^2).

title: Container With Most Water
questionSummary: Given n non-negative integers representing vertical lines, find two lines that together with the x-axis form a container containing the most water.
solutionSummary: Use the two-pointer approach. Place pointers at the beginning and end of the array. Compute the area, update the max area, and move the pointer pointing to the shorter line inward. Time complexity is O(N).

title: Count and Say
questionSummary: Generate the n-th term of the count-and-say sequence. The sequence is defined recursively, starting with '1' and describing the count of consecutive digits of the previous term.
solutionSummary: Iterate from 1 to n. For each term, scan the previous string, count consecutive identical characters, append the count and character to a StringBuilder, and update the term. Time complexity is O(length of terms).

title: Repeated DNA Sequences
questionSummary: Given a DNA sequence string, find all the 10-letter-long sequences (substrings) that occur more than once in the DNA molecule.
solutionSummary: Use a HashSet to record all 10-character substrings encountered during a single iteration. If a substring is already in the set, add it to a second result set. Time complexity is O(N) using substring hashing.

title: Add Two Numbers
questionSummary: You are given two linked lists representing two non-negative numbers. The digits are stored in reverse order. Add the two numbers.
solutionSummary: Iterate through both lists simultaneously, keeping track of the carry, and construct a new linked list with the sums.

title: Reorder List
questionSummary: Given a singly linked list, reorder it to: L0→Ln→L1→Ln-1→L2...
solutionSummary: Find the middle of the list, reverse the second half, and merge the two halves alternately.

title: Linked List Cycle
questionSummary: Given a linked list, determine if it has a cycle in it.
solutionSummary: Use Floyd's Cycle-Finding Algorithm (slow and fast pointers). If the fast pointer meets the slow pointer, there is a cycle.

title: Copy List with Random Pointer
questionSummary: A linked list is given such that each node contains an additional random pointer. Return a deep copy of the list.
solutionSummary: Use a HashMap to map original nodes to copied nodes, or interleave the copied nodes with the original nodes to avoid extra space.

title: Merge Two Sorted Lists
questionSummary: Merge two sorted linked lists and return it as a new list.
solutionSummary: Use a dummy head node and iteratively append the smaller node from the two lists until one is exhausted, then append the remaining list.

title: Merge k Sorted Lists
questionSummary: Merge k sorted linked lists and return it as one sorted list.
solutionSummary: Use a PriorityQueue (Min-Heap) to keep track of the smallest current node among all the lists, or use divide and conquer.

title: Remove Duplicates from Sorted List
questionSummary: Given a sorted linked list, delete all duplicates such that each element appear only once.
solutionSummary: Iterate through the list. If the current node's value equals the next node's value, skip the next node.

title: Partition List
questionSummary: Given a linked list and a value x, partition it such that all nodes less than x come before nodes greater than or equal to x.
solutionSummary: Create two separate lists for nodes less than x and nodes greater or equal to x, then connect them.

title: LRU Cache
questionSummary: Design and implement a Least Recently Used (LRU) Cache that supports get and put operations in O(1) time complexity.
solutionSummary: Use a HashMap combined with a Double Linked List. The HashMap provides O(1) node lookup. The Double Linked List tracks usage order: move accessed elements to the head, and evict from the tail on capacity overflow.

title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
questionSummary: Given the heads of two singly linked lists, return the node at which the two lists intersect. If they do not intersect, return null.
solutionSummary: Calculate the lengths of both lists. Move the pointer of the longer list forward by the difference in length. Then traverse both lists in lockstep until the pointers meet, which is the intersection node.

title: Java PriorityQueue Class Example
questionSummary: Demonstrates the usage of Java's PriorityQueue class, illustrating how elements are ordered according to their natural ordering or by a custom Comparator.
solutionSummary: Provides an implementation example showcasing standard queue operations (offer, poll, peek) and custom sorting behavior for elements.

title: Binary Tree Preorder Traversal
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, return the preorder traversal of its nodes' values (root, left, right).
solutionSummary: Iterative Approach: Use a Stack. Push the root. In a loop, pop the node, record its value, and push its right child followed by its left child (so left is processed first). Time complexity is O(N).

title: Binary Tree Inorder Traversal
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, return the inorder traversal of its nodes' values (left, root, right).
solutionSummary: Iterative Approach: Use a Stack. Initialize current pointer to root. Push current node and move left until null. Then pop, record value, set current pointer to right child, and repeat. Time complexity is O(N).

title: Iterative Binary Tree Postorder Traversal
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, return the postorder traversal of its nodes' values (left, right, root).
solutionSummary: Iterative Approach: Use two Stacks, or a single stack with a 'last visited' pointer. For the two-stack method, push root to first stack, then pop and push to second stack, pushing its left and right children to the first stack. Pop all from second stack.

title: Validate Binary Search Tree
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, determine if it is a valid binary search tree (BST).
solutionSummary: Recursively check if each node's value falls within a valid range [min, max], updating the range for left and right subtrees.

title: Flatten Binary Tree to Linked List
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, flatten it to a linked list in-place, following pre-order traversal.
solutionSummary: Use a recursive approach or a stack to keep track of right children while re-wiring left children to the right.

title: Path Sum
questionSummary: Given a binary tree and a sum, determine if the tree has a root-to-leaf path such that adding up all the values along the path equals the given sum. 5 / \ / / \ 11 13 4 75 PathSum / \ \ 7 2 1
solutionSummary: Add all node to a queue and store sum value of each node to another queue. When it is a leaf node, check the stored sum value. 5 4 8 11 13 4 7 2 1 For the tree above, the queue would be: - - - - - - - - .

title: Construct Binary Tree from Inorder and Postorder Traversal
questionSummary: Given inorder and postorder traversal of a tree, construct the binary tree.
solutionSummary: The last element in postorder is the root. Find its index in inorder to divide into left and right subtrees, then recurse.

title: Convert Sorted Array to Binary Search Tree
questionSummary: Given an array where elements are sorted in ascending order, convert it to a height balanced BST.
solutionSummary: Use the middle element of the array as the root, and recursively construct the left and right subtrees from the two halves.

title: Convert Sorted List to Binary Search Tree
questionSummary: Given a singly linked list where elements are sorted in ascending order, convert it to a height balanced BST.
solutionSummary: Use a slow and fast pointer to find the middle of the list, make it the root, and recursively build subtrees.

title: Minimum Depth of Binary Tree
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, find its minimum depth.
solutionSummary: Use BFS (Level Order Traversal) to find the first leaf node, or use recursion to find the minimum of left and right subtree depths.

title: Binary Tree Maximum Path Sum
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, find the maximum path sum. The path may start and end at any node in the tree.
solutionSummary: Use recursion. For each node, compute the max path sum of left and right children. Update the global max with left + right + node.val.

title: Balanced Binary Tree
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, determine if it is height-balanced.
solutionSummary: Recursively compute the height of subtrees. If the difference in heights is > 1 or a subtree is unbalanced, return -1 to indicate it's not balanced.

title: Symmetric Tree
questionSummary: Given a binary tree, check whether it is a mirror of itself (i.e., symmetric around its center).
solutionSummary: Use recursion. A tree is symmetric if the left subtree is a mirror of the right subtree. Compare left-child's left with right-child's right, and left-child's right with right-child's left.

title: Clone Graph Java
questionSummary: Given a reference of a node in a connected undirected graph, return a deep copy (clone) of the graph.
solutionSummary: Use a depth-first search (DFS) or breadth-first search (BFS) traversal. Use a HashMap to map original nodes to their cloned counterparts, ensuring each node is cloned exactly once and neighbor connections are correctly copied. O(N) time and space.

title: How Developers Sort
questionSummary: Analyzes sorting paradigms in Java, explaining when and why the JDK uses different sorting algorithms (such as Dual-Pivot Quicksort and Timsort) for primitives and objects.
solutionSummary: Details the performance characteristics and design tradeoffs of Java's Arrays.sort() and Collections.sort(), highlighting stability requirements.

title: Merge Sort LinkedList
questionSummary: Sort a linked list in O(n log n) time using constant space complexity.
solutionSummary: Use Merge Sort. Find the middle of the list using slow/fast pointers, recursively sort both halves, and then merge them.

title: Quicksort Array
questionSummary: Implement Quicksort for an array.
solutionSummary: Pick a pivot, partition the array such that elements smaller are on the left and larger on the right, and recursively apply to sub-arrays.

title: Sort a linked list using insertion sort
questionSummary: Sort a linked list using insertion sort.
solutionSummary: Use a dummy head node. Iterate through the original list and insert each node into its correct sorted position in the new list.

title: Maximum Gap
questionSummary: Given an unsorted array, find the maximum difference between successive elements in its sorted form. The algorithm should run in linear time and space.
solutionSummary: Use Bucket Sort (pigeonhole principle). Divide elements into buckets based on min/max values. The maximum gap must occur between the maximum of one bucket and the minimum of the next non-empty bucket. Time complexity is O(N).

title: Iteration vs. Recursion
questionSummary: Explores the fundamental differences between recursion and iteration using the factorial function (N!). Discusses how recursion builds a chain of operations (linear recursion), while iteration maintains state in a fixed number of variables.
solutionSummary: Recursion uses call stack frames to keep track of deferred operations, leading to O(N) memory complexity for linear recursion. In contrast, iteration executes with a constant number of variables, using O(1) auxiliary space.

title: Edit Distance
questionSummary: Given two words, find the minimum number of steps required to convert word1 to word2 (insert, delete, replace).
solutionSummary: Use Dynamic Programming. dp[i][j] stores the min operations to match word1[0..i] to word2[0..j].

title: Single Number
questionSummary: Given an array of integers, every element appears twice except for one. Find that single one.
solutionSummary: Use XOR bitwise operation. XORing a number with itself results in 0, so XORing all elements leaves the single number.

title: Single Number II
questionSummary: Given a non-empty array of integers, every element appears three times except for one, which appears exactly once. Find that single one.
solutionSummary: Optimal Approach (Bit Manipulation): Maintain two variables ones and twos. For each number, update twos with bits appearing twice, and ones with bits appearing once. Filter out bits that appear three times.

title: Twitter Codility Problem Max Binary Gap
questionSummary: Get maximum binary gap. For example, 9's binary form is 1001, the gap is 2.
solutionSummary: Iterate through the bits. Keep track of the distance between consecutive 1s and update the maximum gap found.

title: Number of 1 Bits
questionSummary: Write a function that takes an unsigned integer and returns the number of '1' bits it has (also known as the Hamming weight).
solutionSummary: Iterate 32 times, shifting the number and checking the last bit. Alternatively, use Brian Kernighan's algorithm: repeatedly clear the lowest set bit using n &= (n - 1) until n becomes 0, counting the steps.

title: Reverse Bits
questionSummary: Reverse bits of a given 32-bit unsigned integer in Java.
solutionSummary: Iterate 32 times. Shift the result to the left, bitwise OR it with the last bit of the input number, and shift the input number to the right. Return the resulting integer.

title: Permutations
questionSummary: Given a collection of numbers, return all possible permutations.
solutionSummary: Use Backtracking (DFS). Swap elements to generate permutations, or build permutations by adding available elements to a temporary list.

title: Permutations II
questionSummary: Given a collection of numbers that might contain duplicates, return all possible unique permutations.
solutionSummary: Sort the array first. Use Backtracking and skip duplicates by checking if the current element equals the previous one.

title: Permutation Sequence
questionSummary: The set [1,2,3,...,n] contains a total of n! unique permutations. Find the kth permutation sequence.
solutionSummary: Use math. Calculate factorials to determine which block the kth permutation falls into, picking digits iteratively.

title: Generate Parentheses
questionSummary: Given n pairs of parentheses, write a function to generate all combinations of well-formed parentheses.
solutionSummary: Use Backtracking (DFS). Add an open parenthesis if available, and add a close parenthesis if there are unclosed open parentheses.

title: Reverse Integer
questionSummary: Reverse digits of an integer.
solutionSummary: Iteratively extract the last digit using modulo 10 and append it to the reversed number, checking for overflow before updating.

title: Palindrome Number
questionSummary: Determine whether an integer is a palindrome. Do this without extra space.
solutionSummary: Reverse the second half of the number and compare it to the first half, handling negative numbers by returning false.

title: Pow(x, n)
questionSummary: Implement pow(x, n).
solutionSummary: Use Exponentiation by Squaring. Recursively calculate pow(x, n/2) and square it, multiplying by x if n is odd.
